summary: White-label partners feature fully tested. All 12 backend tests pass (100%). Fixed bug in digital card API that prevented partner_branding from being returned. Frontend correctly displays partner branding on More page (banner + footer) and uses partner primary_color for tab bar tint.

partner_crud_list.details: Returns list of all white-label partners with Calendar Systems present

partner_crud_get.details: Returns full partner details including all branding fields

partner_crud_create.details: Creates new partner with all fields, returns created doc with _id

partner_crud_update.details: Updates partner fields, verified persistence with GET

partner_assign_org.details: Assigns organization to partner, returns 404 for invalid partner

login_partner_branding.details: Returns partner_branding object when user's store has partner_id

digital_card_branding.details: Returns partner_branding after fix - was missing due to early return

partner_by_slug.details: Public endpoint returns partner by slug

frontend_more_banner.details: Partner banner shows 'Calendar Systems' with logo placeholder and 'Powered by' text

frontend_tab_tint.details: Tab bar active tint uses partner primary_color (#E87722 orange)

admin_white_label_page.details: Shows Calendar Systems partner card with Edit/Orgs/Delete actions

bugs_fixed.issue: Early return at line 56-79 prevented partner_branding from being added to response
bugs_fixed.fix: Removed duplicate return statement, allowing partner_branding logic to execute

seed_data_creation: Calendar Systems partner pre-seeded with ID 69a10678b8e991776ed5df19
context_for_next_testing_agent: White-label partners feature complete. Partner CRUD at /api/admin/partners/. Login returns partner_branding when store has partner_id. Digital card API also returns partner_branding (bug fixed). Frontend More page shows partner banner and footer. Tab bar tint color uses partner's primary_color. Admin page at /admin/white-label for managing partners.
mocked_api: None. All APIs live against local MongoDB.
